explanation: The natural language states that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. The most directly related atomic proposition is 'testPowerSwitchPass', which represents a successful test confirming the power switchover works. The typical LTL way to capture such a requirement is to globally require that, whenever the self-test is performed, this switchover must be confirmed as working (as indicated by 'testPowerSwitchPass'). If the specification is about the self-test mode as a phase (which we could represent if we had an atomic proposition such as 'SelfTestMode'), but since such an atomic proposition is absent, the property becomes: it is always required, during system execution, that the test for power switchover passes. Thus, the LTL formula is: G testPowerSwitchPass. This expresses that at all times, the test for switchover from external to internal power works (as required by the self-test procedure). Previous (potential) LTL properties could incorrectly allow the test to not pass at some point, or not treat this as a hard requirement.

The requirement states that the self-test procedure should confirm the switchover from external to internal power works. Among the atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly indicates successful confirmation of the power switchover test as required in the self-test procedure. A natural translation is that, globally (throughout system behavior), if the system is in self-test mode ('SelfTestMode'), then it should eventually be the case that the power switchover test passes ('testPowerSwitchPass'). This captures that, whenever the self-test is performed, it cannot complete without confirming the switchover works as intended. Previous LTL formulas might not tightly link the passage of the self-test with the necessity of the specific switchover test passing; thus, this property ensures that whenever self-test is run, the confirmation adequacy is linked to the specific 'testPowerSwitchPass' atomic proposition.

The given natural language requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. From the provided atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' and 'selfTestPass' are relevant. The property should state that, globally, whenever the self-test passes, it must have confirmed (i.e., include as part of the passing) the switchover from external to internal power working. In terms of LTL, this means that globally, 'selfTestPass' should imply that the power switch test has passed. This captures the requirement by ensuring that the switchover functionality is confirmed as part of passing the self-test.

The requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. Among the atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' indicates the successful test of the switchover, and 'selfTestPass' indicates that all self-test steps are successful. To capture the requirement, we want the property to state: if the self-test procedure as a whole passes (selfTestPass), then the confirmation of the switchover (testPowerSwitchPass) must have occurred as part of it. In other words, it is never the case that the self-test as a whole passes without the successful switchover confirmation in the process. This is captured in LTL as: G(selfTestPass -> F testPowerSwitchPass). This ensures every time the self-test as a whole passes, at some point (possibly earlier or at the same time), the switchover from external to internal power was confirmed to work. Any LTL that does not relate selfTestPass and testPowerSwitchPass would not adequately capture the requirement.

The input specifies that the self-test procedure must confirm the switchover from external to internal power works. The atomic proposition best aligned with this is 'testPowerSwitchPass', which directly indicates that the test for switchover from external to internal power was successful. Therefore, the LTL formula should state that whenever the self-test process passes as a whole ('selfTestPass'), it must be the case that the power switch test has also passed ('testPowerSwitchPass'). This ensures the self-test includes successful confirmation of the switchover. A strong formalization is 'G(selfTestPass -> testPowerSwitchPass)', meaning globally, if the self-test is reported as passed, then the power switch test must also have been passed. This formula ensures the requirement is captured strictly, as any self-test pass without power switch confirmation would violate the property. There are no earlier formulas given, so no deficiencies to address.

The requirement 'The self-test procedure shall confirm the switchover from external to internal power works.' can be mapped to requiring that the specific step "testPowerSwitchPass" (successfully confirming the power switchover) occurs as part of the self-test. The atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' most closely matches this, indicating success. To ensure it is 'confirmed by the self-test procedure', the self-test must eventually (or globally, as appropriate) cause 'testPowerSwitchPass' to occur. The most direct LTL translation is 'Globally, if SelfTestMode holds, then eventually testPowerSwitchPass holds.' This way, during any self-test mode, the confirmation of the switchover must eventually happen. No numerical comparison is needed, and this directly ensures the requirement is met.

The natural language input requires that the self-test procedure confirms the switchover from external to internal power works. The atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' is defined as the success of this test. Therefore, the correct LTL property should express that whenever the self-test is passed ('selfTestPass'), it implies that the power switch test for switchover success ('testPowerSwitchPass') has also succeeded. This ensures the confirmation is included in the self-test process. Previous translations may not capture this condition if they do not link the self-test pass to the power switch test pass, or if they merely state that the test passes at some point without correlation to the self-test outcome.

The natural language requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm the switchover from external to internal power works. From the provided atomic propositions, 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly indicates that the system has confirmed that the power switchover works as intended. To express that the self-test procedure shall confirm this, it is reasonable to require that whenever the self-test passes, the switchover test must also have passed. This can be captured by the property: G(selfTestPass -> testPowerSwitchPass). This means that globally, whenever all self-test steps pass ('selfTestPass' is true), it must be the case that the switchover from external to internal power worked ('testPowerSwitchPass' is true), thus ensuring that the self-test procedure includes and confirms this functionality. Previous properties might miss explicitly tying the self-test passing to confirmation of the power switchover, but this property does so concretely and directly.

The requirement is that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. The atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' is defined as indicating the success of that specific test. Therefore, the LTL property should capture that, in the self-test procedure, the confirmation (i.e., pass) of the power switch test event eventually happens. Assuming 'SelfTestMode' indicates being in the self-test procedure, a typical safety requirement is that 'if SelfTestMode occurs, then eventually testPowerSwitchPass occurs.' This is captured by the property: G(SelfTestMode -> F testPowerSwitchPass). This ensures that whenever the self-test mode is active, it is always the case that eventually the switchover test for power source passes. No previous LTL property is given to correct, but alternatives that require 'testPowerSwitchPass' globally (G testPowerSwitchPass) would be too strict, and omitting the trigger (SelfTestMode) would not tie the confirmation to the self-test procedure.

The input requirement states that the self-test procedure must confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. Among the atomic propositions given, 'testPowerSwitchPass' directly refers to the switchover success being confirmed by the test. In LTL, we typically expect that upon entering (or during) the self-test procedure, the confirmation occurs at some point. Thus, the LTL 'GF testPowerSwitchPass' specifies that it is always guaranteed that eventually the test for switchover passes (i.e., over all runs and from every point forward during the self-test, the switchover confirmation must eventually occur). Alternatively, if we want to be even more precise and only require this during self-test mode, we could write 'G(SelfTestMode -> F testPowerSwitchPass)'. However, absent further information about mode transitions, 'GF testPowerSwitchPass' is the most direct fit.

The property does not use any numerical comparison operators and employs only temporal and boolean operators with atomic propositions.
